topNews.title: Codex starts encrypting sub-agent prompts
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Codex has begun encrypting prompts used by its sub-agents.  
• Why it matters: This move enhances data security and privacy for users interacting with AI systems.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage this encryption to build trust and differentiate their AI offerings in a competitive market.

• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage this tool to reduce development costs and accelerate time-to-market for their applications.

topNews.summary:
• What happened: PsiQuantum announced plans to build a massive quantum computer using light technology.  
• Why it matters: This innovation could revolutionize computing power and solve complex problems beyond current capabilities.  
• Business opportunity: Companies in tech and data processing sectors could leverage quantum computing advancements for competitive advantages.

topNews.title: OpenAI's Ad Business Is on Pace to Miss Its Own Forecast by 90%, Analyst Says
topNews.rawTextSnippet: OpenAI's Ad Business Is on Pace to Miss Its Own Forecast by 90%, Analyst Says
topNews.summary:
• What happened: OpenAI's advertising revenue is projected to fall 90% short of its forecasts.  
• Why it matters: This significant shortfall raises concerns about the company's growth and sustainability in the ad market.  
• Business opportunity: There may be potential for competitors to capitalize on OpenAI's advertising challenges by attracting its clients.

items.title: Ask HN: Any objective research on which languages are best for AI agents?
items.rawTextSnippet: I keep reading articles that suggest that Rust or Go or some other language is optimal for AI development because of feature X or lack of issue Y. Has there been any actual head to head comparison of this using frontier models? Any benchmarks that purport to measure this?

funding.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
funding.rawTextSnippet: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

dailyBrief: In today's AI landscape, Codex has begun encrypting sub-agent prompts, enhancing security for developers and potentially increasing trust in AI applications. Meanwhile, a new tool allows developers to build and ship Mac and iOS apps without the need for Xcode, streamlining the app development process and reducing time to market. On the quantum computing front, PsiQuantum is advancing its plan to create a massive quantum computer using light, which could revolutionize data processing capabilities across industries. However, OpenAI faces challenges as its advertising revenue is projected to fall 90% short of forecasts, raising concerns about its financial sustainability. Additionally, a new project showcased on Hacker News demonstrates the potential of reinforcement learning in training AI models at a fraction of the cost, highlighting ongoing innovation in the field.
